organization.name: Cliente Exemplo S.A.
organization.current_devops_tools: Source-control, CI runners, data lakehouse, API gateway
organization.key_business_drivers: Modernize platform delivery, accelerate AI-assisted coding adoption, raise PE readiness from LOW to MEDIUM within 12 months.

agentic_platform_engineering.rationale: PE Readiness score 1.48 (LOW) indicates basic capabilities exist but foundational gaps must close before adopting either Three Horizons (Red Hat Developer Hub) or Open Horizons (Backstage). A small Open Horizons pilot may be valuable to learn before scaling, but full IDP adoption should wait until score reaches MEDIUM (≥2.0).

capabilities.name: Assistentes de Codificação IA
evidence_collected: 645 AI coding licenses deployed (out of 2,430 developers)
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1 was assigned because the organization is piloting AI coding assistants with approximately 26% of developers, well below the 50% threshold required for L2. While initial deployment exists, suggestion acceptance is below target and advanced features (Agent Mode, agentic workflows) show zero usage.
h1_initiatives: Deploy enterprise-tier AI coding licenses to 100% of developers
Establish adoption metrics dashboard with weekly reporting
Launch champions program (1 champion per 20 developers)
Mandatory training: 4-hour onboarding for all developers
capabilities.h3_target_label: Autonomous code generation with multi-agent workflows

capabilities.name: Plataforma de Experiência do Desenvolvedor
evidence_collected: AI code review available but not standardized
<10% of pull requests use AI-assisted review
No policy mandates AI review usage
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1 reflects nascent AI code review usage. Tools are available to all developers but adoption is sporadic, with fewer than 10% of PRs leveraging AI assistance. Without a standardized policy or measurable enforcement, the capability remains in pilot phase.
h1_initiatives: Deploy AI code review across all repositories
Define mandatory checks for security and quality
Integrate AI suggestions into PR workflow
capabilities.h3_target_label: Autonomous review with human approval gate

capabilities.name: Gestão do Conhecimento
evidence_collected: AI test generation in pilot for 2 teams
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1.5 reflects piloted but inconsistent AI test generation. Two teams have evidence of consistent usage with measurable coverage improvements, but the practice is not organization-wide. The half-point above L1 acknowledges concrete pilot success.
h1_initiatives: Roll out AI test generation to all teams
Establish coverage targets per service tier

capabilities.name: Automação de Revisão de Código
evidence_collected: Some teams use AI for inline documentation
API documentation auto-generated for 30% of services
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1.5 reflects partial automation: API docs auto-generated for a subset of services, but ADRs and runbooks still manual. The half-point recognizes meaningful automation in production for code-level documentation.
h1_initiatives: Standardize AI doc generation across all services

capabilities.name: Onboarding e Treinamento de Desenvolvedores
evidence_collected: Developer onboarding takes 4-6 weeks to first commit
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1 because onboarding includes basic AI tool exposure but lacks structured ramp-up paths, formal AI skills tracks, or measurable proficiency gates. The 4-6 week time-to-first-commit is well above industry benchmarks.
h1_initiatives: Define AI proficiency levels and assessments
Establish mentor-AI pairing for first 30 days

capabilities.name: Inner Source e Colaboração
evidence_collected: IDE setup takes 4-8 hours per developer
Cloud IDE in pilot for 1 team
Local environment drift causes 20% of new-dev support tickets
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1 reflects the manual, drift-prone state of local environments. While a cloud IDE pilot exists, it is not the default. Setup time and environment-related support tickets indicate significant opportunity for standardization.
h1_initiatives: Standardize dev containers across all repositories
Roll out cloud IDE as default for new hires
Create one-click environment templates per service

capabilities.name: Automação de Documentação
evidence_collected: Technical debt tracking exists but not AI-prioritized
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1 reflects awareness of refactoring needs but lack of AI tooling integration. The organization tracks technical debt manually but has no automated prioritization or AI-assisted modernization in production.
h1_initiatives: Train teams on AI-driven refactoring patterns

capabilities.name: Medição de Produtividade do Desenvolvedor
evidence_collected: DORA metrics tracked at portfolio level
SPACE framework partially adopted (3 of 5 dimensions)
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects established DORA tracking and partial SPACE adoption. Surveys provide qualitative signal, but real-time AI-driven productivity insights are absent.
h1_initiatives: Integrate AI-derived signals into engineering reviews

capabilities.name: Inteligência de Pipeline CI/CD
evidence_collected: Git-based source control across all teams
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1.5 reflects modern source control with inconsistent branching practices. The half-point above L1 recognizes signed-commit enforcement on a meaningful subset of repositories.
h1_initiatives: Mandate signed commits across all repos

capabilities.name: Infraestrutura como Código
evidence_collected: CD pipelines exist for 60% of services
Manual gates remain in production deployments
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects mature CI with partial CD coverage. Pipeline-as-code is standard, but the 40% of services without automated CD and the prevalence of manual production gates prevent advancement to L3.
h1_initiatives: Replace manual gates with automated checks

capabilities.name: Observabilidade e Monitoramento
evidence_collected: Build caching enabled for major repos
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects centralized artifact management with build optimization. SBOM coverage at 50% and lack of org-wide reproducible-build enforcement keep the score below L3.

capabilities.name: Integração de Segurança (DevSecOps)
evidence_collected: Unit test automation in CI for all services
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects strong unit and integration test automation. The lack of automated flaky-test detection and incomplete E2E coverage in main pipelines prevent L3.
h1_initiatives: Move E2E tests to PR pipeline for critical paths

capabilities.name: Estratégias de Release e Implantação
evidence_collected: SAST and SCA in 100% of pipelines
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2.5 reflects mature shift-left security with broad SAST/SCA adoption and enforced secret scanning. The half-point above L2 acknowledges DAST for the majority of public services and threat-modeling pilots.
h1_initiatives: Expand DAST to 100% of public services
Roll out threat modeling tool org-wide

capabilities.name: Automação de Testes
evidence_collected: Feature flags for 40% of new features
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects modern deployment strategies in production but with uneven adoption. Canary at 30% and feature flags at 40% indicate progressive delivery is the exception rather than the rule.
h1_initiatives: Standardize feature flags across all services
Expand canary deployments to 80% of services
Automate rollback decisions based on SLO breaches

capabilities.name: Gestão de Incidentes e SRE
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2.5 reflects strong observability foundations with distributed tracing, centralized logs, and SLO discipline. The half-point above L2 reflects breadth of tracing and SLO adoption. Manual alert tuning prevents L3.
h1_initiatives: Reduce alert noise via intelligent grouping

capabilities.name: Gestão de Artefatos e Pacotes
evidence_collected: On-call rotations exist for tier-1 services
Postmortems written but not always actionable
MTTR varies widely (30 min to 8 hours)
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1.5 reflects basic incident response with significant variability in MTTR. The half-point recognizes consistent on-call coverage for tier-1 services. Lack of formal SRE practice limits L2.

capabilities.name: Gestão de Mudanças e GitOps
evidence_collected: No chaos engineering practice in place
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L0 reflects complete absence of chaos engineering practices. While disaster recovery exercises occur annually, they do not constitute proactive chaos testing.
h1_initiatives: Build chaos testing into pre-prod pipelines
capabilities.h3_target_label: Continuous chaos with AI safety net

capabilities.name: Segurança de Dependências e Cadeia de Suprimentos
evidence_collected: Audit trails captured for 80% of changes
Manual evidence collection for major audits
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects established policy-as-code and broad audit-trail coverage. The manual nature of major audit evidence collection prevents L3.
h1_initiatives: Automate evidence collection for all controls
Integrate compliance checks into CD pipelines

capabilities.name: Arquitetura Cloud-Native
evidence_collected: Legacy monoliths still in production for 30% of workloads
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects significant cloud-native adoption. The remaining 30% of monolithic workloads and the pilot stage of service mesh keep the score below L3.
h1_initiatives: Adopt event-driven architecture for new services
capabilities.h3_target_label: Cloud-native by default with mesh-aware routing

capabilities.name: Gestão de APIs
evidence_collected: Service catalog in early planning phase
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L0.5 reflects early-stage IDP thinking with no production deployment. The half-point recognizes the experimental work by one team. The complete absence of a service catalog or self-service portal keeps the score below L1.
h1_initiatives: Define IDP vision and target architecture
Identify and onboard 3 pilot teams to IDP

capabilities.name: Desenvolvimento de Aplicações IA
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L3 reflects mature Kubernetes practice with multi-cluster federation, broad auto-scaling, and GitOps for configuration. The remaining gap to L4 is largely about AI-driven workload placement and predictive scaling.
h1_initiatives: Optimize resource utilization with predictive scaling

capabilities.name: Plataforma de Dados e Lakehouse
evidence_collected: API gateway in production for external APIs
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects mature API gateway with broad OpenAPI adoption and versioning discipline. The lack of enforced contract testing prevents L3.
h1_initiatives: Mandate OpenAPI specs for 100% of services

capabilities.name: Aplicações Agênticas
evidence_collected: Data mesh principles in pilot for 1 domain
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects modern lakehouse architecture with established ETL. Partial data quality monitoring and the pilot stage of data mesh keep the score below L3.
h1_initiatives: Expand data quality monitoring to all critical pipelines
Roll out data mesh principles to 3 domains

capabilities.name: Gestão de Identidades e Acessos
evidence_collected: MLOps platform deployed for 5 use cases
Model registry and versioning in place
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2.5 reflects mature MLOps for production use cases with versioning and registry. The half-point above L2 recognizes A/B testing for model deployments. Feature store pilot stage prevents L3.
h1_initiatives: GA the feature store for all teams
Deploy model monitoring with drift detection

capabilities.name: Multi-Cloud e Portabilidade
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L3 reflects mature IaC with module standardization, drift detection, and comprehensive testing. The remaining gap to L4 concerns AI-assisted module generation and policy intelligence.

capabilities.name: Desempenho e Escalabilidade
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L2 reflects mature cost discipline with allocation tags and review cadence. The absence of automated rightsizing and predictive cost analytics prevents L3.
h1_initiatives: Establish FinOps as a formal practice

capabilities.name: FinOps e Otimização de Custos
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L1.5 reflects multi-AZ resilience with limited multi-region coverage. The half-point above L1 recognizes consistent multi-AZ for critical services. Inconsistent recovery testing prevents L2.

capabilities.name: Platform Adoption & Engineering Culture
evidence_collected: Internal tech talks and demo days
Platform team has clear charter and product mindset
capabilities.scoring_rationale: Score of L3 reflects mature platform-as-product mindset with active community and feedback loops. The remaining gap to L4 concerns AI-driven platform evolution and predictive platform improvements.
h1_initiatives: Establish platform OKRs with AI baselines

horizons.name: Foundation
horizons.narrative: H1 establishes the foundational platform, governance, and adoption patterns required for transformation at scale.

key_initiatives.title: AI Coding Adoption Foundation
items: Deploy enterprise-tier AI coding licenses to 100% of developers

horizons.name: Scale
horizons.narrative: H2 scales H1 successes across the engineering organization with full IDP adoption and broader agentic workflow integration.

key_initiatives.title: IDP General Availability
items: Establish golden paths for top 10 use cases

key_initiatives.title: Agentic Workflows
items: Implement AI test selection and prioritization

horizons.name: Transform
horizons.narrative: H3 transforms operations into autonomous, self-healing systems with multi-agent coordination across the SDLC.

recommended_actions.h1: Definir baseline e estabelecer métricas de cobertura.
recommended_actions.h2: Expandir piloto para >50% das equipes; instrumentar OKRs.
recommended_actions.h3: Atingir cobertura universal e otimização contínua.

P1.focus_summary: AI coding adoption + dev experience standardization

effort_summary.focus_areas: Agent Mode scale, AI test gen

P3.focus_summary: IDP buildout + cloud-native maturation + AI/ML platform GA

effort_summary.focus_areas: IDP MVP, service mesh, MLOps GA

P1.mitigation: Change management, hands-on training, champions program, executive visibility
Quality monitoring, model tuning, feedback loops
Structured training, pair programming with AI, continuous learning paths

P2.mitigation: Staged rollout, parallel pipelines, automated rollback
AI-aware security scanning, mandatory review, audit trails
Standardize on validated tool catalog, sunset legacy tools per quarter

P3.mitigation: Pilot teams as internal champions, golden paths for top use cases, exec sponsorship
Strangler fig pattern, dedicated modernization team, phased decommission
Quarterly FinOps reviews, effort tier gating, pilot before scale

cross_cutting.mitigation: Steering committee monthly cadence, OKR alignment, business-value reporting
Retention bonuses, career development, public visibility of transformation work
Compliance-as-code, continuous audit, regulatory advisor on steering committee
Multi-vendor strategy where viable, abstraction layers, exit-cost analysis at procurement
Phased rollout, ADKAR framework, recognition of contributors, clear quick-win narrative

immediate_30d: Secure executive sponsorship for Pillar 1 transformation
Procure enterprise AI coding licenses for all developers
Identify 3 pilot teams for AI coding deep-dive
Establish baseline AI coding metrics dashboard
Launch champions program with first cohort of 10 champions
h1_launch_30_90d: Roll out enterprise AI coding to first 50% of developers
Deliver mandatory AI coding training to pilot teams
Standardize dev containers across top 10 repositories
Begin cloud IDE pilot with 1 development team
Publish first weekly AI adoption metrics report
dependencies: Part 2: DevOps Lifecycle transformation alignment for CI integration
Part 3: Application Platform IDP work for consistent dev experience
Part 4: Implementation Guide governance for change management

immediate_30d: Establish formal SRE function with named lead
Inventory existing pipeline maturity per service
Identify priority services for canary + feature flag rollout
h1_launch_30_90d: Achieve 100% CI/CD pipeline coverage gap closure for prioritized services
Deploy AI anomaly detection to first 5 tier-1 services
Run first chaos engineering game day
Launch DevSecOps deepening initiative (DAST 100%, threat modeling)
dependencies: Part 1: Developer Productivity for AI test generation alignment
Part 3: Application Platform observability stack alignment

immediate_30d: Define IDP target architecture and golden-path principles
Inventory existing platform capabilities and gaps
Identify 3 pilot teams for IDP early adoption
h1_launch_30_90d: Onboard first 3 pilot teams to IDP MVP
Begin service mesh deployment to 30% of services
dependencies: Part 1: Developer Productivity adoption metrics for IDP success measurement
Part 2: DevOps Lifecycle observability investments shared with platform

documents: Architectural decision records reviewed across 6 critical services
Service catalog draft and IDP target architecture proposal
Current DevOps tooling inventory with utilization data
dashboards: AI coding usage dashboard (license deployment + acceptance metrics)
SLO compliance dashboards for tier-1 services
interviews: Engineering leadership discovery sessions (CTO, VP Eng, Pillar Leads)
Pillar working group sessions (8 capability deep-dives)
Developer-experience round tables (15 developers across 5 teams)
metrics: DORA metrics: deployment frequency, lead time, change failure, MTTR
AI suggestion acceptance and adoption telemetry
Test coverage and mutation testing rates per repository
governance: Existing engineering policies (branch protection, security scanning)
Risk register entries from previous quarters

implementation_guide_inputs.adkar_notes:
Plano de Mudança ADKAR derivado do Learning Survey:

**Awareness:** Comunicar plano de capacitação consolidado em all-hands; cada dev recebe seu plano personalizado por email.

**Desire:** Tornar visível que workshops têm inscritos pré-validados (não opt-in genérico).

**Knowledge:** Workshops top 5 (do Learning Survey):
1. Copilot Spaces (contexto compartilhado) (5 inscritos)
2. A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol (4 inscritos)
3. Microsoft Foundry — visão geral (4 inscritos)
4. Documentação automática (4 inscritos)
5. Modo Ask (perguntas eficazes) (3 inscritos)

**Ability:** Office hours quinzenal (sem agenda fixa, devs trazem dúvidas práticas).

**Reinforcement:** Métricas de adoção mensais publicadas; reconhecimento dos Champions; revisão trimestral do plano com novo Learning Survey.
